DetectionRule.Name: Autorun Registry Modification for Persistence
DetectionRule.Description: Detects unauthorized modifications to autorun registry keys used for persistence, including Run, RunOnce, and other startup locations. Identifies both registry value changes and creation of new autorun entries.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1547.001 - Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

RemediationSteps.Step: Remove unauthorized autorun entries from registry
Check for associated files referenced in autorun entries
Analyze the executable for malicious content
Reset credentials for accounts that made modifications
Enable auditing of registry autorun modifications
Implement application control to prevent unauthorized persistence

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: User-based: Exclude known administrators during maintenance

DetectionRule.Name: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation
DetectionRule.Description: Detects creation of scheduled tasks for persistence, particularly those with suspicious execution patterns, hidden tasks, or tasks running under SYSTEM context. Identifies both schtasks.exe and direct Task Scheduler API usage.

RemediationSteps.Step: Delete unauthorized scheduled tasks immediately
Check task actions for malicious executables or scripts
Review task triggers and execution schedules
Reset credentials for accounts that created the tasks
Enable auditing of scheduled task creation
Implement least privilege for task creation permissions

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude SCCM and Intune management tasks
Task-based: Exclude known legitimate maintenance tasks

DetectionRule.Name: WMI Event Subscription Persistence
DetectionRule.Description: Detects WMI event subscription persistence mechanisms where attackers create permanent event consumers and filters that execute malicious code when specific events occur. Identifies unusual WMI persistence techniques.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1546.003 - Event Triggered Execution: Windows Management Instrumentation Event Subscription

RemediationSteps.Step: Identify and remove unauthorized WMI event consumers and filters
Use wbemtest or PowerShell to enumerate WMI subscriptions
Check for malicious commands in EventConsumer configurations
Reset credentials for accounts that created WMI subscriptions
Enable WMI activity auditing
Consider disabling WMI if not required for business operations

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude monitoring agents and legitimate admin tools
User-based: Require admin privileges for WMI modifications

DetectionRule.Name: Suspicious Windows Service Creation
DetectionRule.Description: Detects creation of Windows services for persistence, particularly services with suspicious names, running under unusual accounts, or configured to auto-start. Identifies both sc.exe and API-based service creation.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1543.003 - Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service

RemediationSteps.Step: Stop and delete unauthorized services immediately
Investigate the executable or script associated with the service
Check for lateral movement or additional persistence mechanisms
Reset credentials for accounts that created or modified services
Enable service creation auditing
Implement application control to prevent unauthorized service installation

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude authorized software installation and update processes
Exclude system services and Windows components

DetectionRule.Name: Startup Folder Modification for Persistence
DetectionRule.Description: Detects modifications to startup folders (user and common) for persistence mechanisms. Monitors file creation, modification, and deletion in startup directories commonly abused by attackers.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1547.001 - Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Startup Folder

RemediationSteps.Step: Remove unauthorized files from startup folders
Analyze suspicious files for malicious content
Check for additional persistence mechanisms
Reset credentials for affected user accounts
Enable monitoring of startup folder modifications
Educate users about social engineering attacks

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Time-based: Alert on unusual modification patterns
